Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Hmm let me start by carefully reading the normative process description to understand the expected flow. The process should always begin with Receive order followed by Check inventory. After that there are two branches: if inventory is sufficient the flow is Authorize payment Package items Ship order Close order. If inventory is insufficient it should be Cancel order Close order. Key constraints are that Authorize payment must come after Check inventory and before Package items and Ship order must never happen after cancellation.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Now looking at the event log Ill examine each case step by step. Case 1 follows the ideal flow perfectly. Case 2 has Package items before Authorize payment which violates the rule that packaging should only occur after authorization. Case 3 is particularly problematic: after Check inventory and Authorize payment it cancels the order but still proceeds to Ship order which is illogical since shipping after cancellation is explicitly prohibited. Case 4 follows the cancellation branch correctly. Case 5 skips Package items entirely which means items were shipped without being packaged violating the rule that packaging must occur before shipping.

Name: Validation - PE
Text: So the violating cases are 2 3 and 5. Ill explain each violation clearly referencing the normative description and highlighting the specific rules broken.
